module.name: Resolution
module.description: The core mechanic that resolves all checks, attacks, resistances, and outcomes. Everything in the system runs through this: roll d20, add modifier, compare to DC or opposed check.
module.dominant_mechanism: d20 + modifier vs. Difficulty Class or opposed check; degrees of success/failure determine outcome magnitude.

epic.name: Resolve Check
epic.trigger: Any action whose outcome is uncertain — attack, resistance, skill use, power activation
epic.outcome: A pass/fail result with degree, which determines what happens next (damage condition, effect applied, action succeeds)

module.name: Character
module.description: The concept that holds all traits defining a hero or villain: abilities, defenses, skills, advantages, powers, complications, and the point budget that governs their creation and advancement.
module.dominant_mechanism: Power Point budget constrained by Power Level caps; all traits purchased at defined point costs; PL limits attack/defense and effect/resistance trade-offs.

epic.name: Build Character
epic.trigger: Player allocates power points across abilities, skills, advantages, and powers within PL limits
epic.outcome: A valid character sheet with all traits within PL caps and total PP within budget

module.name: Abilities
module.description: The eight core attributes (Strength, Stamina, Agility, Dexterity, Fighting, Intellect, Awareness, Presence) that serve as the foundation for all checks and defenses.
module.dominant_mechanism: Each ability has a rank; rank directly feeds into checks, defenses, and skill bonuses. Defenses (Dodge, Parry, Fortitude, Toughness, Will) are derived from or purchased on top of abilities.

epic.name: Configure Abilities
epic.trigger: Player spends power points to set ability ranks during character creation or advancement
epic.outcome: Ability ranks set; derived defenses and initiative bonus established

module.name: Skills
module.description: Trained proficiencies that enhance ability checks. Each skill is linked to an ability; rank adds to the ability modifier. Skills cover specific domains: combat, social, investigative, technical.
module.dominant_mechanism: Skill bonus = Ability rank + Skill rank. Untrained checks use ability only. Skill Mastery allows routine checks even under pressure.

epic.name: Use Skill
epic.trigger: Player attempts an action covered by a trained skill under uncertain conditions
epic.outcome: Check result compared to DC; success/failure with degree determines outcome

module.name: Advantages
module.description: Minor benefits that grant flat bonuses, special abilities, or modify how specific actions work. Divide into Combat, Fortune, General, and Skill types. Some are ranked; most are not.
module.dominant_mechanism: Each advantage has a type category and specific rule. Combat advantages modify attack/defense tradeoffs. Fortune advantages interact with hero points. No point cost — purchased from PP budget at 1 point each (or per rank).

epic.name: Apply Advantage
epic.trigger: Player selects and uses an advantage during character creation or in play
epic.outcome: Advantage effect applied — combat modifier activated, fortune benefit triggered, or general benefit available

module.name: Powers
module.description: The system of extraordinary effects available to heroes. Powers are purchased Effects with parameters (action, range, duration, cost). Effects are modified by Extras (enhancements) and Flaws (limitations) that adjust per-rank cost.
module.dominant_mechanism: Effect base cost per rank ± modifiers (Extras add, Flaws subtract). Each Effect has: action required, range, duration, resistance check. Power Level limits effect rank vs. resistance check pairing.

epic.name: Build Power
epic.trigger: Player selects an effect and configures it with extras, flaws, and descriptors within PP budget and PL limits
epic.outcome: A power with defined effect, cost, action, range, duration, and resistance

module.name: Effects
module.description: The named power effects that define what a power does: Affliction, Damage, Healing, Move Object, Summon, Teleport, Weaken, etc. Each Effect is a distinct mechanic with its own action, range, duration, resistance, and cost.
module.dominant_mechanism: Each Effect has a canonical parameter set. Resistance check type and DC are Effect-specific. Effect rank determines magnitude. Effects are the leaf nodes of the power system.

epic.name: Activate Effect
epic.trigger: Character uses a power with a specific Effect during play
epic.outcome: Effect resolves against target's resistance; condition or state change applied per degree of success

module.name: Modifiers
module.description: Extras and Flaws that modify power effects. Extras increase per-rank cost and add capability (Area, Affects Others, Accurate, Contagious, etc.). Flaws reduce per-rank cost and add limitations (Limited, Check Required, Tiring, Fades, etc.).
module.dominant_mechanism: Each modifier adds or subtracts from cost per rank. Flat modifiers add/subtract a fixed point total. The result changes what the effect can do or requires of the user.

epic.name: Modify Power Effect
epic.trigger: Player adds extras or flaws to an effect during power construction
epic.outcome: Effect cost per rank adjusted; effect gains enhanced capability or acquires limitation

module.name: Combat
module.description: The structured exchange of attacks, defenses, and conditions that governs conflict. Covers initiative, action types, attack checks, defense checks, damage resistance, conditions, and maneuvers.
module.dominant_mechanism: Attack check (d20 + attack bonus) vs. Defense DC. On hit: resistance check (d20 + Toughness/Dodge/Will/Fortitude) vs. Effect DC. Degrees of failure on resistance determine condition applied. Conditions accumulate.

epic.name: Resolve Combat Exchange
epic.trigger: Character takes an attack action against a target
epic.outcome: Hit or miss determined; if hit, resistance check made; condition applied or worsened based on degree of failure

module.name: Conditions
module.description: Stateful effects on characters that result from combat, powers, or hazards. Conditions have defined mechanical effects (Dazed, Staggered, Incapacitated, etc.) and accumulate or recover in defined ways.
module.dominant_mechanism: Conditions are applied by degrees of failure on resistance checks. Characters recover one step per minute of rest. Conditions modify action availability, defense values, and check bonuses.

epic.name: Apply and Recover Condition
epic.trigger: Character fails a resistance check or recovers between scenes
epic.outcome: Condition applied or stepped up; or condition cleared and character returns to normal function

module.name: Hero Points
module.description: A meta-resource players spend to influence outcomes: re-roll dice, recover conditions, activate stunts, resist compels, or gain advantages in narrative. GM awards them for complications and hero-worthy behavior.
module.dominant_mechanism: Flat resource pool. Spend 1 point for re-roll, recovering from conditions, or power stunts. GM awards on complications triggering. Fortune advantages interact with this pool.

epic.name: Spend Hero Point
epic.trigger: Player spends a hero point at a moment of need or opportunity
epic.outcome: Re-roll accepted, condition cleared, stunt activated, or narrative advantage gained

module.name: Complications
module.description: Character flaws, responsibilities, and liabilities that create story problems. Complications are not mechanical penalties — they are narrative hooks. When a complication creates a genuine problem, the GM awards a hero point.
module.dominant_mechanism: Complications are invoked by the GM in play. Invocation creates a problem for the character (Accident, Enemy, Identity, Weakness, etc.). Player accepts → GM awards hero point.

epic.name: Invoke Complication
epic.trigger: GM invokes a character's complication to create a story problem
epic.outcome: Character faces a meaningful obstacle or hindrance; GM awards a hero point to the player

module.name: Equipment and Gear
module.description: Physical devices, weapons, armor, vehicles, and headquarters available to characters. Equipment is purchased with power points via the Equipment advantage. Devices are powers built into objects.
module.dominant_mechanism: Equipment advantage grants equipment points (5 per rank). Each item has a point cost. Devices are powers with the Removable or Easily Removable flaw reducing PP cost.

epic.name: Equip Character
epic.trigger: Player spends equipment points on gear, vehicles, or headquarters
epic.outcome: Character gains access to equipment with defined capabilities and Toughness ratings

module.name: Gamemastering
module.description: The rules and guidance governing how the GM runs encounters, awards advancement, creates adversaries, and maintains game balance. Covers power level calibration, encounter design, NPC creation, and series frameworks.
module.dominant_mechanism: Power Level as a universal balance dial. NPCs built by same rules as PCs. Encounter difficulty scaled by comparing PL of heroes vs. threats. Series tone shapes what rules apply.

epic.name: Run Encounter
epic.trigger: GM presents a conflict or challenge to the heroes
epic.outcome: Encounter resolved; hero points awarded; advancement or consequences applied

notes: The corpus is the Mutants & Masterminds 3rd Edition Hero's Handbook. It has a clean chapter structure that maps naturally to modules. The deepest mechanical complexity lives in Powers (Effects + Modifiers) — this area likely needs to be split into at least two modules (Effects and Modifiers are distinct enough to warrant separation, as confirmed by the chunk evidence). Resolution is a cross-cutting mechanism that every other module depends on — it should be modeled first. Character is the aggregate root that owns all other traits. The Powers/Effects/Modifiers split reflects a genuine taxonomic distinction: Powers is the structure (arrays, descriptors, cost system), Effects are the named leaf mechanics, and Modifiers are the adjustment system that modifies both. Advantages sit at an interesting boundary — some (Combat) are essentially situational modifiers to resolution; others (Fortune) interact with the Hero Points economy; others (General) are standalone capabilities. The Advantages module may need an intermediary grouping.
